mnemonics: LIFO: Last-In-First-Out. Imagine a stack of plates. The last plate you put on top is the first one you take off.
PUSH-POP-PEEK: PUSH puts an item on the stack (like adding a plate). POP removes the top item (taking the top plate). PEEK just looks at the top item without removing it.
TOP TRACKER: Always remember to update the 'top' pointer. Think of it as the index of the last plate you added.
keyPoints: An array is a static, contiguous block of memory. The stack grows and shrinks within this fixed size.
A variable (often named 'top') is used to track the index of the most recently added element.
The 'stack underflow' error occurs when you try to POP or PEEK from an empty stack (top == -1).
The 'stack overflow' error occurs when you try to PUSH an element onto a full stack (top == size - 1).
Operations like PUSH, POP, and PEEK are O(1) constant time operations.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check for stack underflow before a POP operation, leading to runtime errors.
Forgetting to check for stack overflow before a PUSH operation, causing data to be written outside the array bounds.
Incorrectly updating the 'top' pointer. For example, decrementing 'top' after a POP but before retrieving the value.
Assuming the stack is 1-indexed instead of 0-indexed, leading to off-by-one errors.
